title: HTTP/1.1 Message Framing and Length Precedence · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent protocol team receives different fragmentation, peer, trust, timing, and failure constraints and transfers http/1.1 message framing and length precedence into a new RFC 9112 message-body length decision table. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of RFC 9112 message-body length decision table with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply the RFC 9112 message-body length precedence in order using request or response role, method, status, transfer coding, content length, and connection closure.
Forbid content framing for HEAD responses, 1xx, 204, successful CONNECT, and other context-specific cases while retaining metadata meaning.
Validate transfer-coding order, require final chunked coding for request framing, and close after ambiguous Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Body Length Precedence
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab protocol team handles case 7942 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to apply framing precedence and close on Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length ambiguity; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply the RFC 9112 message-body length precedence in order using request or response role, method, status, transfer coding, content length, and connection closure.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Body Length Precedence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply the rfc 9112 message-body length precedence in order using request or response role, method, status, transfer coding, content length, and connection closure. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Body Length Precedence, keep Body Length Precedence connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
